environment.notes: intra-region, same AZ as bucket, cold OS cache per run

single_curl_probe.description: single-stream curl of first 2 GiB via presigned URL
single_curl_probe.notes: proves single-stream TCP to this key tops out <100 MB/s; multipart is the unlock

key_finding: S3 per-object throttling caps single-key GETs at ~135 MB/s regardless of client concurrency (8-256 all converge). boto3.download_file already uses multipart internally (prior 67 MB/s baseline was wrong). Our rewrite ships with identical throughput, progress callbacks, typed errors, and 26 tests — but the speedup story is flat at 1.0x on a single key. The real unlock is sharding the .thaw snapshot across N S3 keys during freeze, then fetching N keys in parallel (N * 135 MB/s).
next_actions: Freeze-side: split .thaw across N keys (e.g. 4 x 4 GiB) behind a flag
Restore-side: issue ranged GETs per shard in parallel across distinct keys
Stage 2 Rust thaw-cloud crate is NOT the fix here — same S3 ceiling applies to any client
Optional: evaluate boto3 with aws-crt backend for a marginal CRT-vs-urllib3 delta
